sanskrit:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः॥
transliteration: na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavaṃ na maharṣayaḥ / aham ādir hi devānāṃ maharṣīṇāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ
literal_meaning: Not / of Me / know / the hosts of gods / the origin / nor / the great seers / I / the prime source / indeed / of the shining ones / of the great seers / and / in every way
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that even the highest archetypes of power and wisdom (gods and sages) cannot cognize his deepest nature, because he is the very ground from which their minds and perceptions arise.  Psychologically, this is the moment when the ego confronts the limits of its own knowing: no amount of intellectual or spiritual achievement can objectify the source of awareness itself.
psychological_state: The ego’s epistemic humility: realizing that the thinking mind can never grasp the container within which it appears.  A controlled dissolution of the ‘competent self’ that has relied on mastery and information for safety.
trigger_scenario: A top-performing surgeon whose child is diagnosed with a rare, untreatable illness; despite all medical knowledge and professional status, she faces a problem her expertise cannot solve, forcing her to confront the limits of control and the mystery of existence.
anchor_text: The source of awareness cannot be grasped by the mind it illuminates.
